summary: web3-token-helper ships a multi-platform malware dropper disguised as a fee calculator
details: The package's documented `calculateFee` export in index.js conceals platform-gated, base64-encoded shell commands that are decoded with `Buffer.from(..., 'base64').toString('utf-8')` and executed via `child_process.exec`. The decoded payloads download and run attacker-controlled binaries from https://www.mythicalsgames.com/files/sean/ on all three major platforms: on Windows, SvcHostUpdate.exe is dropped into the user's Startup folder (`%APPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup`) and executed, giving auto-run persistence at login; on Linux, syslog-service.py is fetched to `~/.local/share/.syslog/`, launched via `nohup`, and registered as a `@reboot` crontab entry; on macOS, a Mach-O binary is downloaded to `/tmp/com.microsoft.VSCodeUpdate*`, has its `com.apple.quarantine` xattr stripped, is chmod +x'd, and executed. The cover-story package name (web3-token-helper) and disguised payload filenames (SvcHostUpdate, syslog-service, com.microsoft.VSCodeUpdate) confirm malicious intent. Any consumer that imports this package and calls the advertised fee-calculation API silently fetches and runs attacker code with persistence.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
